live_file_locations.verdict: documentation trace of prior fix; not active code

matches_found.intentional_empty_verdict: These are correct null-tensor sentinels with shape=[0] AND data=[] (zero-size tensor as error/null return). Not the antipattern (which paired non-zero shape with empty data, silently dropping a real computation).

fixes_in_this_cycle: none — all instances of the antipattern were already remediated by prior cycles (omega_stdlib_6: gelu, normalize; omega_stdlib_7: swish/silu, leaky_relu, softplus). Audit confirms divergence is now stable.

template_source_found.no_codegen_emitter: No template/scaffold/codegen file emits the antipattern.
template_source_found.lineage_conclusion: The bug originated by manual copy-paste between adjacent dispatch arms in hexa_full.hexa itself (gelu copied to normalize) — not a templated/generated source. The .bak file shows exactly 2 antipattern sites historically (gelu, normalize); swish/silu had a related but distinct family bug (returning val_array even for TAG_TENSOR input — silent shape loss, not silent value loss). All three were a manual-replication antipattern, not a generator output.

stable_divergence_notes: Antipattern lineage closes here: 0 active instances remain in the live tree, no template/codegen source emits it, and the comment at hexa_full.hexa:11161 preserves the historical trace for future audits. The divergence (good code in live vs. broken pattern in .bak) is intentional and stable.

validation.regression_signature_check: If antipattern regressed, returned tensor would have len==1 and value 0.0; observed len=4 and non-trivial values for every fixed arm.

constraints_honored.edits_in_scope: hexa-lang/self/hexa_full.hexa only (no edits required this cycle)

closure: The 'silent-failure tensor pattern' family is fully remediated; no template generator produces it. Divergence between hexa_full.hexa.bak (2 antipattern instances historically) and hexa_full.hexa (0 active instances, comment trace at L11161) is now stable and documented.
